title: refactor(gen-image): fold seedream5 into SEEDREAM_VARIANTS table
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Add seedream5 (bytedance/seedream-5-lite) as a 3rd entry in
  SEEDREAM_VARIANTS instead of a standalone handler; widen size unions
  with Seedream5Size = "2K"|"3K" and add opt-in outputFormat to the
  variant config.
- buildPresetInput now spreads output_format only when variant.outputFormat
  is set, so seedream-4.5's strict schema never sees an unknown field.
- callSeedream5API is now a thin wrapper over callSeedreamReplicateAPI;
  seedream5ReplicateModel.ts becomes a ~3-line re-export so the
  createAndReturnImages.ts dispatch import is unchanged.
- Delete the ~140-line duplicate seedream5 handler (aspect-ratio +
  size resolvers were near-identical copies of the shared variants).
- Add seedream5 payload tests (2K at <=2048, 3K at >2048, output_format
  png, no custom-size branch, 14-image cap) plus output_format opt-in
  guards on the 4.0/4.5 payloads.

Risks:
- billed model seedream5 previously had zero tests; this PR adds payload
  coverage before collapsing its handler, so behavior is now pinned.
- medium-risk because seedream5's emitted payload changed source: verified
  identical fields (size 2K/3K, output_format png, sequential disabled,
  max_images 1, 14-image cap, match_input_image default) via the new tests.

_~91 net lines removed · biome ✓ · cd gen.pollinations.ai && npx vitest run test/image/seedreamModel.test.ts ✓_

gist.headline: Spring cleaning the Seedream code
gist.blurb: Cleaned up the image generation backend by folding Seedream5 into our shared variants table. Less duplicate code means a healthier, happier hive!
gist.summary: Refactored the Seedream5 image generation handler to use the shared SEEDREAM_VARIANTS table, removing ~140 lines of duplicate code and adding payload test coverage.
gist.impact: This internal cleanup makes the image generation backend easier to maintain and ensures consistent behavior for the bytedance/seedream-5-lite model.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot organizing files or folding blueprints, while the Nomnom creature eats the deleted duplicate code.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny tool belt, neatly folding glowing blue blueprints on a wooden workbench. The round tan nomnom creature happily eats a stack of discarded duplicate code papers nearby. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
